subcommand so HTTP frameworks start a listener.

) so frameworks such as Mojolicious start an HTTP server. The working directory is set to
and all relative paths (including
WithLocalLib
and cpanfile discovery) resolve against it.

summary.text: Configures the Perl application to use Carton as its package manager. Carton manages dependencies via

remarks.text: Carton only supports project-level dependency installation via
will throw because Carton does not support installing individual modules. To install Carton, you may use "cpanm Carton".

summary.text: Configures the Perl application to use cpanm (App::cpanminus) as its package manager instead of the default cpan. Call this before
to change how packages are installed.

members.name: WithLocalLib
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<TResource> PerlAppResourceBuilderExtensions.WithLocalLib<TResource>(this IResourceBuilder<TResource> builder, string path = "local")

summary.text: Configures the Perl application to use a local::lib directory for module isolation. Sets
environment variables so that modules are resolved from and installed into the local directory.

remarks.text: These ensure modules are resolved from and installed into the local directory without requiring system-level permissions.

children.text: If the active package manager is
cpan
(the default), it is automatically switched to
cpanm
since
cpan
does not support the
--local-lib
flag. While it is possible to use
cpan
with Local::Lib it is complicated to model in Aspire.

path.text: The path to the local::lib directory. Relative paths are resolved against the resource's working directory (
), not the AppHost project. Rooted paths (e.g.,

members.name: WithPackage
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<TResource> PerlAppResourceBuilderExtensions.WithPackage<TResource>(this IResourceBuilder<TResource> builder, string packageName, bool force = false, bool skipTest = false)

summary.text: Adds a Perl package (module) to be installed before the application starts. Uses the configured package manager: cpan by default, or cpanm if

remarks.text: A child installer resource is created that runs before the main application starts. If the module is already installed (verified via
), installation is skipped. The installer uses the active package manager —

force.text: , force installation even if the module is already installed or tests fail.

packageName.text: The name of the Perl module to install (e.g., "Mojolicious", "DBI").

members.name: WithPerlbrewEnvironment

summary.text: Configures the Perl application to use a specific perlbrew-managed Perl version. This resolves the Perl executable from the perlbrew installation and updates the resource's command and environment variables so that all subsequent operations use the specified Perl version.

children.text: Perlbrew is Linux-only. On Windows, an
is thrown with a recommendation to use
Berrybrew
When combined with
, modules are installed into the local directory rather than the perlbrew tree, keeping the perlbrew installation clean and enabling per-project module isolation.

TResource.text: The type of the Perl application resource.

children.text: This resource allows Perl applications (scripts, web servers, APIs, background services) to run as part of a distributed application. The resource manages the Perl executable, working directory, and lifecycle of the Perl application.

Perl applications can expose HTTP endpoints, communicate with other services, and participate in service discovery like other Aspire resources. They support automatic OpenTelemetry instrumentation for observability when configured with the appropriate Perl packages.
This resource supports various Perl execution environments including:

appDirectory.text: The working directory for the Perl application. Perl scripts and modules will be resolved relative to this directory. This is typically the root directory of your Perl project containing your main script and any local modules.

executablePath.text: The path to the Perl executable. This can be:

description.text: A command on the PATH: "perl" or "myperl"

executablePath.text: (resolved from PATH), or a path to a perlbrew-managed Perl installation's bin directory.

name.text: The name of the resource in the application model.

description.text: Add a Perl web application using Mojolicious or Dancer2:

members.name: .ctor
members.signature: public PerlAppResource.PerlAppResource(string name, string executablePath, string appDirectory)

children.text: This resource allows Perl applications (scripts, web servers, APIs, background services) to run as part of a distributed application. The resource manages the Perl executable, working directory, and lifecycle of the Perl application.

Perl applications can expose HTTP endpoints, communicate with other services, and participate in service discovery like other Aspire resources. They support automatic OpenTelemetry instrumentation for observability when configured with the appropriate Perl packages.
This resource supports various Perl execution environments including:

appDirectory.text: The working directory for the Perl application. Perl scripts and modules will be resolved relative to this directory. This is typically the root directory of your Perl project containing your main script and any local modules.

executablePath.text: The path to the Perl executable. This can be:

description.text: A command on the PATH: "perl" or "myperl"

executablePath.text: (resolved from PATH), or a path to a perlbrew-managed Perl installation's bin directory.

name.text: The name of the resource in the application model.

description.text: Add a Perl web application using Mojolicious or Dancer2:

summary.text: A resource that represents a package installer for a perl module.

packageManager.text: The package manager to use for the installation, typically CPAN, CPANM (Cpan Minus), or Carton.

workingDirectory.text: The working directory to use for the command.

members.name: .ctor
members.signature: public PerlModuleInstallerResource.PerlModuleInstallerResource(string name, string packageManager, string workingDirectory)

summary.text: A resource that represents a package installer for a perl module.

packageManager.text: The package manager to use for the installation, typically CPAN, CPANM (Cpan Minus), or Carton.

workingDirectory.text: The working directory to use for the command.

summary.text: Defines the type of entrypoint for a Perl application. Supported entrypoint types currently include Script, API, Module, and Executable.

enumMembers.name: Executable
enumMembers.description: A direct executable file to run (e.g., a PAR-packed binary or compiled Perl application).

enumMembers.name: Script
enumMembers.description: A Perl script file to execute directly (e.g., "main.pl", "app.pl").

enumMembers.name: Module
enumMembers.description: A Perl module to run as the main application (e.g., "MyApp::Main").

enumMembers.name: API
enumMembers.description: A Perl API script to listen for HTTP requests.

summary.text: Identifies the Perl package manager used for module installation.

enumMembers.name: Cpan
enumMembers.description: The standard CPAN client (cpan). Default for all Perl resources.

enumMembers.name: Cpanm
enumMembers.description: App::cpanminus (cpanm). A lightweight alternative to the full CPAN client. Enabled via WithCpanMinus().

enumMembers.name: Carton
enumMembers.description: Carton — a Perl module dependency manager using cpanfile and lock files. Enabled via WithCarton().

summary.text: Gets the executable name used to invoke this package manager on the command line.

packageManager.text: The package manager value to map to an executable name.
